Original text in two columns extends over conjoint folios. Only the bottom half of each original folio is preserved

Belongs to the same manuscript as Codex Sinaiticus Rescriptus St. Petersburg i = Syr. no. 16; Codex Sinaiticus Zosimi Rescriptus (CSZR) III = Oslo, Martin Schøyen MS 35; 37; Sinai Georgian NF M 19. Reconstructed order is as follows: 1. Sokoloff, (eds.), A corpus of Christian Palestinian Aramaic. Vol. I: The Christian Palestinian Aramaic version of the Old Testament and Apocrypha from the early period, Groningen: Styx, 1997 ; C. Müller-Kessler, “Piecing together Christian Palestinian Aramaic Texts under Georgian Manuscripts (St Petersburg, NLR, Syr. 16; Sinai, Georg. NF 19, 71; Oslo, Martin Schøyen MS 35, 37; Princeton, Garrett MS 24; Göttingen, Syr. 17, 19, 23, 25),” Digital Kartvelology 1 (2022), pp. 1-25 [https://adh.ge/en/digital-kartvelology/] ; Müller-Kessler 2014, pp. 263-309 ; Müller-Kessler 2022, pp. 1-25
Reconstructed folio order, ff. 1r, 8v, 8r, 1v

Original text in two columns extends over conjoint folios. Only the top half of each original folio is preserved. Fols. 1r+8v represent the top half of the original folio in two columns. Fols. 8r+1v represent the top half of the original folio in two column

For a possible reconstruction of the sequence of this text, see Müller-Kessler 2014, pp. 263-309: 1. Syr. no. 16, fol. 108r Deut. 6:4-5a; 7b-10a    Syr. no. 16, fol. 108v Deut. 6:11b-12a; 14b-16a 2. Syr. no. 16, fol. 127r Deut. 7:25-26; Job 6:1b-4a    Syr. no. 16, fol. 127v Job 6:5b-13a 3. Syr. no. 16, fol. 109r Job 7:21b; Is. 40:1-5a    Syr. no. 16, fol. 109v Is. 40:6b-7 4. Syr. no. 16, fol. 63/68r Job 9:12b-23a    Syr. no. 16, fol. 63/68v Job 9:23b-34 5. Syr. no. 16, fol. 38/106r Deut. 13:6b-12a    Syr. no. 16, fol. 38/106v Deut. 13:12b-17a 6. Syr. no. 16, fol. 107/48r Deut. 13:17b; Job 21:1-12a    Syr. no. 16, fol. 107/48v Job 21:12b-22 7. Syr. no. 16, fol. 41/60r Prov. 9:1-9a    Syr. no. 16, fol. 41/60v Prov. 9:9b-11; Is. 40:9-12a 8. MS 35, fol. 27r Dan. 3:24b-26; 28-30b    MS 35, fol. 27v Dan. 3:32-33a; 35b-37 9. MS 35, fol. 62/63r Prov. 1:15b-19; Jer. 1:11-13a    MS 35, fol. 62/63v Jer. 1:13b-17; Ps. 26(27):7 10. Georgian NF 71, fol. 1r/8v Ex. 4:3–5:1; 3–4a    Georgian NF 71, fol. 1v/8r Ex. 5:6–7a; 6b–10
Probably belongs to Codex Sinaiticus Rescriptus (St. Petersburg/Oslo) (= Codex Sinaiticus Rescriptus (St. Petersburg b/Codex Sinaiticus Zosimi Rescriptus I) Lectionary
Description of undertext by Christa Müller-Kessler, Sebastian Brock

note.value: Description by Christa Müller-Kessler, Sebastian Brock
Bibliography: Müller-Kessler and Sokoloff ; Desreumaux 1997
Reconstructed folio order, ff. 4v, 5r, 4r, 5v

Fols. 4v+5r represent upper outer part, with ends of lines and left margin of the original recto with a single column. Fols. 4r+5v represent upper part of the original verso with beginnings of lines and right margin. Since the bottom of the original folio is lost, the total number of lines per page is not discernible
Description of undertext by Christa Müller-Kessler, Sebastian Brock
Bibliography: Müller-Kessler and Sokoloff ; Desreumaux 1997

location.repository: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai

note.value: Sewn quire (quaternion); this was quire 2 of the original manuscript as indicated by quire signature
